---
title: "LRPITT13: SES-adjusted ITT effect of group assignment on word reading (W)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPITT13 is the **SES-adjusted** ITT analysis for word reading (EWRSWR) (`ewrswr`) — the SES-robustness companion to LRPITT10. It uses the uniform LRPITT spec plus linear adjustment for parental education (`MUMEDUPOST16`, `DADEDUPOST16`) and age first exposed to books (`AGEBOOKS`). Requesting these covariates triggers complete-case dropping, so the fit runs on the SES-complete subset; LRPITT14 is the matched unadjusted comparator on the same rows, so LRPITT13 vs LRPITT14 isolates the SES adjustment with the sample held fixed. The estimand is the total effect of randomised assignment $G$ on $W_{\text{post}}$ (RCT phase only).

::: {.callout-note title="Identification and sign convention"}

Under the locked causal DAG, $G$ is a randomised root, so the ITT effect $\tau$ is
identified by the **empty adjustment set**. The own baseline, linear age, and the SES adjusters, are
**precision terms only** — they sharpen $\tau$ but are not what licenses the causal
interpretation. **Sign convention:** $G = 1$ is the intervention arm, so a
**positive** $\tau$ means the intervention raises the outcome.

:::

## Model

$$
\eta_i = \alpha + \tau\, G_i
       + \gamma_{\text{own}}\, \text{logit}_*(W_{\mathrm{pre},i}, N)
       + \gamma_A\, A_{\text{std},i} + \sum_{c \in \text{SES}} \gamma_c\, z(c_i),
$$

with Beta-Binomial likelihood $W_{\text{post},i} \sim \text{BetaBinomial}(N, \mu_i\kappa, (1-\mu_i)\kappa)$,
$\mu_i = \sigma(\eta_i)$, $N = 79$. Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.
The SES adjusters enter as linear $\gamma_c$ terms (the same weak $\text{Normal}(0, 0.3)$ prior as cross-baseline couplings); HSGPs are off by default.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_results_itt.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
